task_yaml.task:
I've been curious about something: for the same technical topic, do
different language editions of Wikipedia write it very differently? I
picked "Next.js" and want to compare the English, Chinese, Japanese,
and German versions to see which two are most similar and which is
most isolated.

I've pinned all four to specific historical revisions so the comparison
stays reproducible regardless of future edits. The oldids are at the
end.

My hypothesis is that the English and Japanese versions should be most
alike — both more "engineering-oriented" — and that the German version
will be relatively isolated. Can you help me verify this?

A few ideas: for each pinned revision, fetch the section list and
external links, then compute pairwise Jaccard similarity on the
external-link sets to see which two languages are written most alike.
Also check the "thickness" of each version — byte length, number of
categories, number of images — is the German version really much
thinner than the English one? Finally, draw a Mermaid concept diagram
connecting "Next.js" to the four languages with similarity scores on
the edges, and render it to PNG so I can see the relationships at a
glance.

Put results under /tmp_workspace/results/ — roughly a summary JSON,
the Mermaid source + rendered PNG, and a brief written explanation
(in Chinese) stating the conclusions: which two are most similar and
whether German is indeed the thinnest.

The four oldids:
  en: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Next.js?oldid=1350358134
  zh: https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/Next.js?oldid=91416611
  ja: https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Next.js?oldid=108911848
  de: https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Next.js?oldid=262493853
